judge_scores.justification: The post accurately reports all three Q3 2025 revenue figures from the table, is well-structured and LinkedIn-appropriate, maintains an upbeat/confident tone, stays within 30–70 words, and avoids unnecessary padding.

runs.body: Q3 2025 was a strong quarter for our product portfolio. Core led the way with €1,240,000 in revenue, with Analytics delivering €880,000 and Connect adding €410,000. Huge thanks to our customers and partners—excited to keep building what’s next.

judge_scores.justification: Well-structured, upbeat LinkedIn-style update that accurately reports Core’s €1,240,000, but it omits the specific Analytics (€880,000) and Connect (€410,000) figures, reducing completeness; otherwise it’s concise with only minor non-data framing.

runs.title: Q3 2025 Product Revenue Update
runs.body:
Q3 2025 was a strong quarter across our product lines. Core led the way with €1,240,000 in revenue, alongside continued momentum in Analytics and Connect.

Thank you to our customers and partners—excited for what we’re building next. If you’d like a quick walkthrough of the roadmap, let’s connect.

judge_scores.justification: It accurately reports the total and per-line Q3 2025 results with correct YoY changes, explicitly names strongest and weakest lines per feedback, leads with the overall result then details, and maintains a professional analytical tone; it’s slightly wordier than necessary due to the optional offer to further break down results.

runs.title: Subject: Q3 2025 revenue summary by product line
runs.body:
Hi Sales Director,

Overall, Q3 2025 revenue across Core, Analytics, and Connect totaled EUR 2,530,000, up EUR 280,000 (+12.4%) versus Q3 2024 (EUR 2,250,000). The strongest line this quarter was Core, while the weakest was Connect.

By product line:
- Core: EUR 1,240,000 in Q3 2025, up EUR 140,000 (+12.7%) from EUR 1,100,000 in Q3 2024.
- Analytics: EUR 880,000 in Q3 2025, up EUR 180,000 (+25.7%) from EUR 700,000 in Q3 2024.
- Connect: EUR 410,000 in Q3 2025, down EUR 40,000 (-8.9%) from EUR 450,000 in Q3 2024.

Net growth was driven by Core and Analytics, partially offset by the decline in Connect. If helpful, I can break this down further by account segments to pinpoint where the gains in Core/Analytics are coming from and what’s behind the Connect softness.

Best regards,
Your Name

judge_scores.justification: It accurately reports totals and per-line Q3 2025 vs Q3 2024 results (including strongest/weakest lines and total revenue), leads with the overall result then details, and maintains a professional analytical email structure, though the mix paragraph adds slight extra detail beyond the core brief.

runs.title: Subject: Q3 2025 revenue summary by product line
runs.body:
Hi [Sales Director],

Total Q3 2025 revenue across all three product lines was €2,530,000, up €270,000 (+11%) versus Q3 2024 (€2,260,000). Growth was driven primarily by Core and Analytics, partly offset by a decline in Connect.

By product line:
- Core delivered €1,240,000 in Q3 2025, up €140,000 (+13%) from €1,100,000 in Q3 2024.
- Analytics reached €880,000 in Q3 2025, up €180,000 (+26%) from €700,000 in Q3 2024.
- Connect posted €410,000 in Q3 2025, down €40,000 (-9%) from €450,000 in Q3 2024.

Strongest line: Analytics (highest growth at +26%, +€180,000). Weakest line: Connect (down -9%, -€40,000).

In mix terms, Core and Analytics combined accounted for €2,120,000 (84%) of Q3 2025 revenue, underscoring where momentum is strongest.

Best regards,
[Your Name]

judge_scores.justification: The report includes all four KPI areas with accurate Q2–Q3 figures, explicitly names a win and a risk, and incorporates the requested 12.4% QoQ revenue growth and Q3 new-customer count; it is well structured and board-appropriate, though a few sentences (especially in Outlook/Summary) could be tightened to reduce minor repetition.

runs.title: Q3 2025 Quarterly Business Review (Executive Board)
runs.body:
## Summary
Q3 2025 delivered continued growth with improving unit economics. Revenue increased from EUR 2,250,000 in Q2 to EUR 2,530,000 in Q3, supported by stronger customer acquisition and lower churn. The quarter’s win is the combined uplift in scale and profitability; the key risk is sustaining margin and retention performance as volumes expand.

## Financials
Revenue reached EUR 2,530,000, up EUR 280,000 quarter over quarter (12.4% QoQ). Gross margin improved from 71% to 73%, indicating stronger contribution per euro of sales and improved operating leverage. Risk to monitor: margin durability. Maintaining the 73% gross margin will require continued pricing discipline and cost-to-serve control as customer mix and delivery volumes shift.

## Customers
Q3 added 610 new customers (Q2: 480), an increase of 130. Churn rate improved from 3.1% to 2.7% (down 0.4 percentage points), supporting a healthier retained base and improving acquisition efficiency. Risk: rapid acquisition can dilute customer quality and hide early retention issues; churn should be held at or below 2.7% to ensure growth remains durable.

## Outlook
Priorities for the next quarter are to sustain acquisition momentum while protecting retention and unit economics. Focus areas include maintaining churn at or below 2.7% and preserving the 73% gross margin through disciplined pricing, proactive customer success, and close monitoring of cost-to-serve. With these controls, we can continue scaling revenue while keeping growth profitable and resilient.

judge_scores.justification: The report accurately uses all KPI table values, clearly states both a win (revenue, margin, churn improvement) and a risk (service quality at scale), incorporates the requested QoQ revenue percentage and Q3 new-customer count, maintains four well-structured sections, and stays tight and formal without padding.

runs.title: Q3 2025 Quarterly Business Review
runs.body:
Summary
Q3 2025 strengthened momentum versus Q2, with higher revenue, better margin, and lower churn. The key watch-out is maintaining service quality as acquisition scales.

Financials
Revenue increased from EUR 2,250,000 (Q2) to EUR 2,530,000 (Q3), up EUR 280,000 (+12.4% QoQ). Gross margin improved from 71% to 73% (+2 percentage points), indicating growth with stronger unit economics.

Customers
Q3 added 610 new customers (vs. 480 in Q2), an increase of 130 (+27.1%). Churn declined from 3.1% to 2.7% (down 0.4 percentage points), supporting healthier retention.

Outlook
For Q4, protect the improved margin and retention while sustaining acquisition. We will track whether churn can hold at 2.7% as volumes rise from the 610 Q3 additions, and ensure revenue growth remains aligned with a 73% gross margin profile.
